- **QUEUED**: Payment request is queued for processing

items.title: Payment Request
items.description:
A Payment Request represents an intent to process a payment transaction. It contains all the necessary 
information to initiate payment processing on a device and track the payment lifecycle from creation 
through completion.

**Lifecycle Overview:**
1. Created with initial status (RECEIVED/QUEUED)
2. Sent to device for customer interaction
3. Customer completes payment flow on device
4. Transaction(s) created and associated with payment request
5. Final status reached (COMPLETED/FAILED)

**Key Concepts:**
- **Async Processing**: Payment requests are processed asynchronously with status updates via callbacks
- **Device Integration**: Payment requests are sent to physical payment devices (terminals, tablets, etc.)
- **Activity Tracking**: All interactions and events are logged in the activities array
- **Transaction Association**: Completed payments generate transaction records linked via transactionIds

**Integration Patterns:**
- Use callbacks for real-time status updates during async processing
- Poll the GET endpoint if callbacks are not configured
- Monitor activities array for detailed payment flow tracking
- Handle race conditions gracefully (payment may complete while processing other operations)

transactionRefNum.description: Optional merchant reference number for tracking (UUID format)

deviceId.description: Device identifier for this payment request (UUID or URN format)

paymentInstructions.description: Non-sensitive payment instructions passed to the device (e.g. skip signature, force PIN entry)

endpoints.description:
Create a new payment request for the specified store. This is an **asynchronous operation**.

**Async Processing:**
- Returns `202 Accepted` with immediate `paymentRequestId` for tracking
- Processing happens in the background with status updates via callbacks
- Use callbacks (if configured) or polling GET endpoint for completion status
- Initial status will be `RECEIVED` or `QUEUED`

**Idempotency:**
- The `X-Request-Id` header is used for idempotency
- Duplicate requests with the same `X-Request-Id` within 24 hours will return the existing payment request
- This prevents duplicate payment requests due to network issues or retries

**Required Properties:**
- `amount` - Payment request amount
- `type` - Payment request type

**Optional Properties:**
- `deviceId` - Device identifier for this payment request
- `paymentInstructions` - Payment instructions
- `notes` - Additional notes about the payment request
- `references` - External system references
- `callbackConfig` - Configuration for async status notifications

**Idempotency:** This endpoint supports idempotency via the X-Request-Id header to prevent duplicate payment requests.

parameters.description: Unique request identifier for idempotency and tracing. Used to prevent duplicate payment requests.

endpoints.description:
Retrieve complete information for a single payment request including:
- Payment request details and current status
- Amount information
- Associated transaction IDs
- Device and store information
- References and metadata

parameters.name: paymentRequestId
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the payment request

parameters.name: X-Request-Id
parameters.description: Unique request identifier for idempotency and tracing. Used to prevent duplicate payment requests.

schema.title: Payment Request
schema.description:
A Payment Request represents an intent to process a payment transaction. It contains all the necessary 
information to initiate payment processing on a device and track the payment lifecycle from creation 
through completion.

**Lifecycle Overview:**
1. Created with initial status (RECEIVED/QUEUED)
2. Sent to device for customer interaction
3. Customer completes payment flow on device
4. Transaction(s) created and associated with payment request
5. Final status reached (COMPLETED/FAILED)

**Key Concepts:**
- **Async Processing**: Payment requests are processed asynchronously with status updates via callbacks
- **Device Integration**: Payment requests are sent to physical payment devices (terminals, tablets, etc.)
- **Activity Tracking**: All interactions and events are logged in the activities array
- **Transaction Association**: Completed payments generate transaction records linked via transactionIds

**Integration Patterns:**
- Use callbacks for real-time status updates during async processing
- Poll the GET endpoint if callbacks are not configured
- Monitor activities array for detailed payment flow tracking
- Handle race conditions gracefully (payment may complete while processing other operations)

type.description: Type of payment request indicating the transaction operation (AUTHORIZE, SALE, REFUND, etc.)

transactionRefNum.description: Optional merchant reference number for tracking (UUID format)

deviceId.description: Device identifier for this payment request (UUID or URN format)

paymentInstructions.description: Non-sensitive payment instructions passed to the device (e.g. skip signature, force PIN entry)
paymentInstructions.title: Dictionary

status.description: Current status of the payment request in its lifecycle
status.title: Payment Request Status

notes.description: Optional notes about the payment request

items.description: A universally unique identifier (UUID) in [RFC-4122 format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4122).

transactionIds.description: Transaction IDs created from this payment request (populated after processing)

authentication.description:
Optional authentication configuration for callback requests.
If not provided, no authentication headers will be sent.
HMAC authentication is recommended for production environments.
authentication.title: Callback Authentication

oneOf.title: HMAC Authentication
oneOf.description: HMAC authentication using shared secret for payload signing

value.description:
Shared secret key for payload signing.

When using HMAC, the service sends fixed standard headers:
- X-Signature: HMAC signature of the payload
- X-Timestamp: Unix timestamp when request was sent
- X-Request-Id: Unique request identifier for tracing

oneOf.title: Bearer Token Authentication
oneOf.description: Bearer token authentication using Authorization header

value.description: Bearer token for Authorization header

oneOf.title: API Key Authentication
oneOf.description: API key authentication using custom or default header

value.description: API key value for custom header

headerName.description:
Custom header name for API key authentication.
Defaults to 'X-API-Key' if not specified.

payloadConfig.description: Configuration for what data to include in callback payloads
payloadConfig.title: Callback Payload Configuration

includeFields.description:
Array of field names to include in callback payload.

Real Fields (PaymentRequest properties):
- paymentRequestId, type, transactionRefNum, storeId, deviceId, amount
- paymentInstructions, notes, references, transactionIds, links, activities
- createdAt, updatedAt

Virtual Fields (computed/expanded):
- 'transactions': Expands transactionIds to full transaction objects (only virtual field currently supported)

legacyMode.description:
Schema version control for virtual field expansions:
- true: Use v1 transaction schema for 'transactions' virtual field
- false: Use v2 transaction schema for 'transactions' virtual field (default)
Only affects virtual fields; real PaymentRequest fields are unaffected.

items.title: Payment Request Status
items.description:
Current status of the payment request in its lifecycle.

**Status Flow:**
- **RECEIVED**: Initial status when payment request is created
- **QUEUED**: Payment request is queued for processing
- **IN_PROGRESS**: Payment is being processed on the device
- **COMPLETED**: Payment successfully processed and transactions created
- **FAILED**: Payment failed due to error or customer cancellation

events.description:
Array of payment request status values that should trigger callback notifications.
Events correspond to PaymentRequestStatus transitions.

Available Events (from PaymentRequestStatus):
- RECEIVED: When payment request is initially received
- QUEUED: When payment request is queued for processing
- IN_PROGRESS: When payment request processing begins
- COMPLETED: When payment request processing is successfully completed
- FAILED: When payment request processing fails

endpoints.description:
Add a new activity entry to a payment request for tracking customer and device interactions.

**Common Use Cases:**
- Device events: "PAYMENT_REQUEST_RECEIVED", "PAYMENT_POPUP_DISPLAYED", "CARD_READER_ACTIVATED"
- Customer interactions: "CUSTOMER_ENTERED_TIP", "PIN_ENTRY_STARTED", "SIGNATURE_REQUESTED"
- Transaction flow: "CUSTOMER_APPROVED_AMOUNT", "CUSTOMER_CANCELLED_TRANSACTION", "PAYMENT_METHOD_SELECTED"
- Processing events: "SENT_TO_PROCESSOR", "AUTHORIZATION_RECEIVED", "RECEIPT_PRINTED"

**Required Properties:**
- `type` - Activity type identifier (e.g., "CUSTOMER_ENTERED_TIP")
- `timestamp` - When the activity occurred (ISO 8601 format)

**Optional Properties:**
- `data` - Additional context data (e.g., tip amount, card type, error details)

**Notes:**
- Activities are append-only and cannot be modified once created
- Use the actual occurrence time, not when you're logging the activity
- Maintain consistent activity type naming across your integration

endpoints.summary: Get all activities for a payment request
endpoints.description:
Retrieve all activities for a specific payment request in chronological order.

**Use Cases:**
- View complete payment flow history
- Debug payment processing issues
- Audit customer interactions and device events
- Analyze payment request lifecycle and timing

**Filtering Options:**
- Filter by activity type(s) to focus on specific events
- Filter by time range to analyze specific periods
- Use pagination for large activity logs

**Response Details:**
- Activities are returned in chronological order (oldest first)
- Each activity includes type, timestamp, and optional data
- Pagination is supported for large result sets

**Note:** Activities are immutable - this endpoint is read-only.

parameters.name: paymentRequestId
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the payment request

parameters.name: X-Request-Id
parameters.description: Unique request identifier for idempotency and tracing. Used to prevent duplicate payment requests.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Filter activities after this time (ISO 8601 format)

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: Filter activities before this time (ISO 8601 format)

pageToken.description: Token for retrieving the next page of results. Use this value in the pageToken query parameter for the next request.

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

name.description: The human-readable, unique name of the error.

correlationId.description: Internal identifier used for correlation purposes.

message.description: The message that describes the error.

informationLink.description: The URI for detailed information related to this error for the developer.

details.description: An array of additional details about the error. Required for client-side `4XX` errors.

items.title: Error Details
items.description: The error details. Required for client-side `4XX` errors.

field.description: The field that caused the error. If the field is in the body, set this value to the JSON pointer to that field. Required for client-side errors.

value.description: The value of the field that caused the error.

location.description: The location of the field that caused the error. Value is `body`, `path`, or `query`.
